# bellwether

> Every number dated and weighed before it leaves the silo.

A point-in-time archive of 12 US and global agricultural-intelligence sources, stored
exactly as published, served to AI agents over MCP — with every figure cited to its
source document and release date, every direction computed rather than eyeballed, and
every forecast filed in advance into an append-only ledger that grades itself when the
report lands.

This is an explainer for bellwether by Francy Lisboa — it takes a newcomer from
"never seen it" to "ready to implement".

## What it is
bellwether: silo core sample — a steel auger bored down through a grain silo, exposing
dated strata that were never disturbed after they fell.

## The measured claim
On a backtest window whose resolving releases all postdate the model's training cutoff
(so the answers cannot have been memorised), bellwether called the direction of the next
WASDE print correctly in 13 of 23 graded questions, where the archive's own climatology
expects about 7. Exact Poisson-binomial p = 0.007. A naive momentum desk on the identical
questions and referee scores about zero skill, as a null should.

## The honest limit
The direction is measured and skilled. The CONFIDENCE is not yet trustworthy: the system
states roughly 70% and is right roughly 57% of the time. Take the direction; do not size
a decision on the percentage. Every figure behind a call traces to a real dated document
— the defect is over-trust in its own reasoning, not fabrication.

## Out of corpus, by design
No futures prices, basis, spreads, term structure, weather forecasts, or trade-consensus
surveys. These are refused by name rather than improvised.

## For your AI
A downloadable knowledge pack (RVF vector KB + MCP server) ships with this page so your
agent can search the source too: https://francyjglisboa.github.io/bellwether-explainer/bellwether-knowledge-pack.zip

## Links
- Live explainer: https://francyjglisboa.github.io/bellwether-explainer/
- Source repository: https://github.com/FrancyJGLisboa/bellwether
- Backtest protocols and raw per-call results: eval/ in the repository
- AI knowledge pack: https://francyjglisboa.github.io/bellwether-explainer/bellwether-knowledge-pack.zip
